_README: Annotated reference config. Copy to config.json and edit. The actual
config.json shipped with the repo is a stripped-down safe default —
Fields starting with _ are documentation only and ignored by the loader.
Field-level comments are inline as `_comment` keys.

system.name: PenDonn
system._comment: log_level: DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR. INFO is the right default.

wifi._comment: Two physical paths are supported: dual external adapters (production), or single onboard adapter (RPi Zero 2 W only — see config.rpi_zero2w.json). Interface NAMES are unreliable across USB reseats; populate the *_mac fields and interface_manager will resolve the iface by MAC at startup. Get the MACs with `ip link show`.

allowlist._comment: SSIDs the daemon is allowed to attack. Renamed from `whitelist` in Phase 2A — the old name is still accepted (logs a deprecation warning). Semantics flipped from skip-list to target-list at the same time. With strict=true (default + safe), daemon ONLY targets these SSIDs. With strict=false (unrestricted), the list is ignored and every visible AP is fair game — requires safety.armed_override or preflight refuses to start.

cracking._comment: cowpatty is the primary engine on Raspberry Pi: reads .pcapng natively, pure CPU, no OpenCL. aircrack-ng handles legacy .cap files. john as last resort. hashcat is intentionally omitted — local hashcat + PoCL segfaults on ARM64; planned as a remote-hashcat feature.

enumeration._comment: Runs nmap + plugins against networks once cracked. nmap_timing: T0 (paranoid) ... T5 (insane). T4 is reasonable on a stable wired-target. Use T2 if you need to be quiet.

plugins._comment: Plugins under ./plugins/ are loaded dynamically. SECURITY: any .py in this dir is exec'd as root by plugin_manager — keep the dir 0700 root-owned. See docs/SAFETY.md.

web._comment: host: 127.0.0.1 = loopback only (recommended). 0.0.0.0 = expose on LAN — only if basic_auth is enabled. secret_key: leave empty to auto-generate (will warn on every restart); installer will populate config.json.local with a persistent value.

display._comment: type: waveshare_v4 (2.13in 250x122 e-paper, RPi 4/5) | ssd1306 (128x64 OLED, RPi Zero 2W) | none. refresh_interval below 30s causes ghosting/burn-in on e-paper.

safety._comment: SSH-lockout protection. Defaults are intentionally strict — see docs/SAFETY.md before disabling. armed_override=true is required (and acceptable) for RPi Zero 2 W single-interface mode where you've accepted SSH loss.
